title: Compliant vertebral attachment device
abstract: In one general aspect, a vertebral attachment device can include an attachment member having a curved shape defining a concave surface, a flex structure included in the attachment member, and an anchor member coupled to the attachment member and protruding from the concave surface.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A vertebral attachment device, comprising: an attachment member having a curved shape defining a concave surface; a flex structure included in the attachment member; and an anchor member coupled to the attachment member and protruding from the concave surface. 2. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member has a first arm portion and a second arm portion. 3. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member has a first arm portion disposed on one side of the flex structure and a second arm portion disposed on a second side of the flex structure. 4. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member has a first arm portion having a first length from an end of the first arm portion and the flex structure, the attachment member has a second arm portion having a second length from an end of the second arm portion and the flex structure that is different from the first length. 5. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the flex structure defines a recess in the attachment member. 6. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the flex structure is at a location offset from a center of the curved shape of the attachment member. 7. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the flex structure is included on a same side of the attachment member as the anchor member. 8. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member has a length greater than a width of the attachment member. 9. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member defines a convex surface on a side of the attachment member opposite a side of the concave surface, the vertebral attachment device further comprising: a coupling mechanism coupled to the convex surface of the attachment member. 10. The vertebral attachment device of claim 9, wherein the coupling mechanism defines an opening. 11. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, further comprising: a locking mechanism. 12. The vertebral attachment device of claim 11 , wherein the locking mechanism a locking arm. 13. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member defines a convex surface on a side of the attachment member opposite a side of the attachment member defining the concave surface, the curved shape of the attachment member defining a curvature, the vertebral attachment device further comprising: a coupling mechanism coupled to the convex surface of the attachment member such that a line intersecting a center of the curvature and the flex structure also intersects the coupling member. 14. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member defines a convex surface on a side of the attachment member opposite a side of the attachment member defining the concave surface, the curved shape of the attachment member defining a curvature, the vertebral attachment device further comprising: a coupling mechanism coupled to the convex surface of the attachment member such that a line intersecting a center of the curvature and the flex structure does not intersect the coupling member. 15. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the attachment member is biased to the curved shape defining the concave surface, the attachment member having an arm portion configured to be moved such that a radius of curvature of the concave surface is increased. 16. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the anchor member protrudes from the concave surface in a first direction, the attachment member having an arm portion configured to be moved in a second direction substantially opposite the first direction. 17. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1, wherein the anchor member is a first anchor member that has a diameter, the vertebral attachment device further comprising: a second anchor member adjacent the first anchor member without an intervening anchor member, the first anchor member being separated from the second anchor member by a distance greater than the diameter. 18. The vertebral attachment device of claim 17, wherein the distance is at least 2 times the diameter. 19. The vertebral attachment device of claim 1 , wherein the vertebral attachment is monolithically formed. 20. A method, comprising: inserting a vertebral attachment device into a body of a patient, the vertebral attachment device including: an attachment member having a curved shape defining a concave surface, a flex structure included in the attachment member, and an anchor member coupled to the attachment member and protruding from the concave surface; and 

 moving the attachment member from a flexed configuration to at least a partially flexed configuration after the inserting such that the anchor member contacts a vertebra within the body of the patient. 21. The method of claim 21 , wherein the anchor member contacts an anterior portion of the vertebra. 22. The method of claim 21 , wherein the moving the attachment member to a flexed configuration is performed before the inserting. 23. The method of claim 21 , wherein the moving the attachment member to a flexed configuration is performed after the inserting.
